principle: Serve AVIF with WebP fallback via <picture> element — AVIF cuts file size 50% vs JPEG while <picture> guarantees full browser compatibility
explanation: X.perf|image_format_stack: AVIF(50%_smaller_than_jpeg) -> best_compression; WebP -> fallback_Safari<16+older_Android; JPEG/PNG -> final_fallback; implement_via_: + + ; Next.js_next/image -> automatic_format_negotiation; NO:serve_jpeg_only_when_tooling_supports_avif NO:webp_without_avif_as_primary; eg: "" >> ""

principle: Declare srcset with breakpoint descriptors and set fetchpriority='high' on the LCP image — missing srcset wastes mobile bandwidth, missing priority delays the largest paint
explanation: X.perf|responsive_image_lcp: single_src -> full_size_on_mobile(bandwidth_waste); srcset: 480w+800w+1200w_descriptors + sizes_attribute; LCP_image -> fetchpriority='high' + _in_; only_ONE_fetchpriority_high_per_page; NO:srcset_without_sizes_attribute NO:fetchpriority_on_below_fold_images; eg: "" >> ""

principle: Compress the LCP image below 100 KB and preload it in <head> — lazy-loading the hero image is the single most common LCP regression pattern
explanation: X.perf|lcp_image_budget: LCP_image_target <100KB(AVIF_or_WebP); preload: _in_; host_on_CDN_with_edge_caching; good_LCP: <2.5s; NO:loading='lazy'_on_hero_image(delays_discovery) NO:LCP_image_as_CSS_background(not_preloadable); if_responsive_srcset: use_imagesrcset+imagesizes_on_preload_link; eg: "" >> " + in head"

principle: Always declare explicit width and height on every — omitting dimensions causes layout shift as images load, directly worsening CLS score
explanation: X.perf|cls_image_dimensions: _without_width+height -> browser_cant_reserve_space -> layout_shift -> CLS_penalty; set_width+height_matching_natural_dimensions; CSS_aspect-ratio:auto_uses_these_for_space_reservation; for_responsive: width+height_attrs + CSS_max-width:100%;height:auto; NO:_CSS_only_sizing_without_attrs NO:width_height_0_or_1_as_placeholder; CLS_target:<0.1; eg: "" >> ""
